description_long: This house features a striking Tudor Revival architectural style, characterized by its steeply pitched, dark gabled roof and prominent half-timbered detailing on the upper walls. The structure includes two tall, conical towers with multi-paned windows, adding a castle-like appearance. The exterior walls are a combination of light-colored stucco and stonework at the base, with wooden trim accentuating the geometric patterns. There is a small covered entryway with steps leading up to the main door, and a wooden balcony on one side. Multiple chimneys rise from the roof, and the windows are arranged in both symmetrical and vertical strip patterns. The overall design suggests a spacious, multi-story residence with a blend of decorative and functional elements.
description: A large Tudor Revival house with steep gabled roofs, two conical towers, half-timbered walls, stone base, and a wooden balcony.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway, two prominent towers, half-timbered facade, and stone base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tall conical tower, stonework, and the depth of the roof structure.
description_view_2: Rear/front view similar to view 0, with clear visibility of the balcony and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the second tower and the stone and stucco wall details.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered entryway with steps, wooden door, and decorative half-timbered overhang
